messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.

The search result indicates that "East Indian Trading Company" is most likely referring to the "East India Company" (wd:Q83164), as it is the most prominent entity with that name and is associated with British colonial activities in India. 

Step 2: Now I will search for the property that indicates when the East India Company was established. I will look for properties related to "founding," "establishment," or "date of foundation" in the context of the East India Company.

I will use the `search_property` function to find relevant properties.

tool_calls.result:

1. inception (wd:P571 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "founding date"), also known as "built", "commenced on date", "commencement date", "completed", "composed", etc.:
  - time when an entity begins to exist; for date of official opening use P1619
2. notable work (wd:P800 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "founded"), also known as "artwork", "author of", "bibliography", "books by", "creator of", etc.:
  - notable scientific, artistic or literary work, or other work of significance among subject's works
3. date of incorporation (wd:P10786 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "date incorporated", "incorporated", "incorporated on date", "incorporation date", "year incorporated":
  - date when company, organization or city was incorporated
4. foundational text (wd:P457 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "founding document"), also known as "charter", "constitution", "constitutive text", "created by", "created in document", etc.:
  - text through which an institution or object has been created or established
5. location of formation (wd:P740 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "founded in"), also known as "comes from", "formation location", "formation place", "formed in", "founded in", etc.:

6. founded by (wd:P112 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "begun by", "co-founded by", "cofounded by", "co-founder", "cofounder", etc.:
  - founder or co-founder of this organization, religion, place or entity
7. time of earliest written record (wd:P1249 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date of first written record"), also known as "date of first written record", "earliest record", "first attestation", "first attested", "first historic record", etc.:

8. time of discovery or invention (wd:P575 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date of discovery"), also known as "date discovered", "date of discovery", "date of invention", "discovered", "discovered on", etc.:

9. date of official opening (wd:P1619 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "inauguration date"), also known as "date of opening", "date opened", "grand opening", "inaugurated", "inauguration", etc.:

10. date of the first one (wd:P7124 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date of first"), also known as "date of first", "date of the first instance", "first instance":
  - qualifier: when the first element of a quantity appeared/took place

content.message:
The most relevant property for determining when the East India Company was established is "inception" (wdt:P571), as it refers to the time when an entity begins to exist. Alternatively, "date of incorporation" (wdt:P10786) is also relevant, as it specifies when a company was legally incorporated.

To confirm, I will search for properties of the East India Company (wd:Q83164) related to its founding or establishment, using `search_property_of_entity`.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. inception (wd:P571 as wdt/p, matched via "founded"), also known as "built", "commenced on date", "commencement date", "completed", "composed", etc.:
  - time when an entity begins to exist; for date of official opening use P1619
2. founded by (wd:P112 as wdt/p), also known as "begun by", "co-founded by", "cofounded by", "co-founder", "cofounder", etc.:
  - founder or co-founder of this organization, religion, place or entity
3. has part(s) (wd:P527 as wdt/p, matched via "formed from"), also known as "amalgamation of", "assembled from", "assembled out of", "composed of", "comprised of", etc.:
  - part of this subject; inverse property of "part of" (P361). See also "has parts of the class" (P2670).
4. described by source (wd:P1343 as wdt/p, matched via "found in"), also known as "dbs", "def in", "defined in", "desc by biography", "desc by encyclopedia", etc.:
  - work where this item is described, in statistical contexts, a methodological note describing the data
5. Freebase ID (wd:P646 as wdt/p, matched via "FID"), also known as "FID", "Freebase", "Freebase identifier", "MID":
  - identifier for a page in the Freebase database. Format: ... starting with "/g/", use Google Knowledge Graph ID (P2671)
6. has effect (wd:P1542 as wdt/p, matched via "cause of"), also known as "caused", "cause of", "causes", "consequence", "effect", etc.:
  - effect of this item
7. headquarters location (wd:P159 as wdt/p, matched via "based in"), also known as "admin HQ", "based in", "garrison", "head office location", "head quarters", etc.:
  - city or town where an organization's headquarters is or has been situated. Use P276 qualifier for specific building
8. owner of (wd:P1830 as wdt/p, matched via "owns"), also known as "item owned", "owns", "owns property", "shareholder of":
  - entities owned by the subject
9. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
10. dissolved, abolished or demolished date (wd:P576 as wdt/p, matched via "ended"), also known as "abolished", "abolishment date", "ceased date", "ceased on", "ceased publication", etc.:
  - point in time at which the subject (organisation, building) ... equipment, "discontinued date" (P2669) for stopping a product

The most relevant property for determining when the East India Company was established is "inception" (wdt:P571), as it directly refers to the time when the entity began to exist.

Now, I will retrieve the value of the "inception" property for the East India Company (wd:Q83164) using a SPARQL query.

The East India Company was established on December 31, 1600.

Final Answer:
The British East India Company was set up on December 31, 1600.
